headline: Inference efficiency techniques led the week as quantization, prompt caching, and training-free methods broke out together around real-time diffusion and enterprise Claude deployments.
body: NVIDIA's video diffusion work paired dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching, and step distillation to cut the 50-step denoising process to as few as one step on Blackwell B200, anchoring breakouts in quantization, prompt caching, and training-free methods. Prompt caching surfaced again in deployment plumbing as AWS Bedrock added Claude Code support with cross-region inference, cost tracking, and content guardrails. Claude itself led on the enterprise side, with large banks deploying it across their organizations and Anthropic hosting a financial services briefing. Google Cloud pushed multiple Agent Development Kit tutorials covering long-running agents, multi-agent collaboration, and Agent2Agent orchestration.

cited_items.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
